survey_title: Campus Walk Frequency and Mental Refreshment During Breaks Survey
survey_description: This survey evaluates how frequently you engage in campus walks during breaks and how mentally refreshed you feel as a result. Answer each question honestly to receive an accurate assessment.
scoring_note: Each answer carries a score (0-4). Lower total scores indicate high walk frequency and strong mental refreshment. Higher scores suggest infrequent walking and low mental recovery.

questions.text: How many times per day do you typically take a walk during academic breaks?
After a short walk around campus, how mentally refreshed do you feel?

options.label: I feel no mental refreshment at all

questions.text: How long do your campus walks during breaks usually last?

options.label: I do not walk during breaks

questions.text: How well can you concentrate on your studies after taking a walk during a break?
Do you feel that walking on campus between classes helps reduce your academic pressure?

options.label: Definitely yes, it greatly reduces pressure
Not really, I still feel pressured
No, it does not help at all

questions.text: How often do you choose to sit indoors instead of walking during your free time on campus?

options.label: Never, I always prefer to walk
Rarely, walking is my first choice
Sometimes I sit, sometimes I walk
I usually sit rather than walk
Always, I always stay seated indoors

questions.text: How does your mood change after a walk through the campus grounds?
How physically active do you feel during a typical academic day on campus?

options.label: Very active, I walk a lot between activities

questions.text: How often do you use campus outdoor spaces (gardens, courtyards, pathways) during breaks?
How do you feel about your ability to manage mental fatigue throughout the academic day?

options.label: I manage it very well through regular movement

questions.text: How often do you feel mentally stuck or unable to process new information during lectures?

options.label: Never, I always feel mentally clear

questions.text: When you feel mentally tired during the day, what do you most commonly do?

options.label: Go for a walk around campus immediately
Take a short walk if time allows
Sit quietly and rest in place
Use my phone or scroll social media

questions.text: How much does the natural environment of your campus (trees, open areas) contribute to your sense of calm?

options.label: It greatly contributes to my calmness
It has almost no effect on me

questions.text: How often do you plan or schedule a walk into your academic day as a deliberate break strategy?

options.label: Always, it is a regular part of my routine
Never, I do not plan walks at all

questions.text: How would you rate your overall mental well-being on days when you walk regularly on campus compared to days when you do not?

options.label: Slightly worse on walking days due to tiredness
I have never noticed any connection

questions.text: How easy is it for you to detach mentally from academic work during a break walk?

options.label: Very easy, I fully switch off from work
Neutral, sometimes I do sometimes I don't
Difficult, my mind stays on coursework
Impossible, I cannot stop thinking about tasks

questions.text: How often do you feel physically restless or tense after sitting for long periods during classes?

options.label: Never, I manage well through regular movement
Almost always after every long session

questions.text: How satisfied are you with the quality of your mental breaks during the academic day?
How often do you walk with peers or classmates during campus breaks as a social-refreshment activity?

options.label: Never, I always stay alone and seated

questions.text: How do you feel your academic performance is affected by the amount of physical movement you get during the day?

options.label: More movement clearly improves my performance
I see no connection between movement and performance
Physical movement negatively affects my studies

psychological_states.label: Excellent Mental Refreshment
psychological_states.description: You walk frequently and experience very high levels of mental refreshment. Your break habits are excellent, and your mind is well-rested and alert throughout the day. No intervention needed.

psychological_states.label: Good Mental Recovery
psychological_states.description: You maintain reasonably good walking habits and benefit from moderate mental refreshment. Your mental energy levels are mostly stable. Continue your current routine and consider adding more outdoor time.

psychological_states.label: Moderate Refreshment — Improvement Recommended
psychological_states.description: Your walking frequency and mental refreshment are at a moderate level. You may experience occasional mental fatigue. It is recommended to incorporate at least one intentional 10-minute walk into each academic day.

psychological_states.label: Low Mental Refreshment — Action Advised
psychological_states.description: You walk infrequently and mental refreshment is low. You likely experience noticeable mental fatigue and reduced concentration. A structured break-walking routine is strongly advised to improve your well-being.

psychological_states.label: Poor Break Habits — Wellbeing at Risk
psychological_states.description: Your break habits are poor and you are experiencing significant mental fatigue due to inactivity. Academic performance may be affected. Consider speaking with a student wellbeing advisor and start a daily movement plan.

psychological_states.label: Critical Sedentary State — Support Needed
psychological_states.description: You are almost entirely sedentary during the academic day and experience very low mental refreshment. This pattern poses a serious risk to your psychological and physical health. Professional wellbeing support is strongly recommended.
